findings.reasoning: R-04 governs advertisements that state a bonus for a bank/deposit account product (e.g., a checking or savings account bonus tied to APY, time requirements, minimum balances). This page is a TurboTax tax-preparation marketing page. It references a 'Full Service $100 Back Offer' and 'Refer a Friend' rewards, but these are not bank account opening bonuses subject to Regulation DD/bonus-advertising disclosure rules -- they are promotional credits/discounts on tax-preparation service fees and referral rewards, not bonuses for opening a deposit account. The only deposit-account-related content on the page is the Credit Karma Money checking account mention ('Get your tax refund up to 5 days early... For Credit Karma Money (checking account): Banking services provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC'), but no bonus is advertised in connection with that account -- it merely describes an early-refund-deposit feature, not a bonus for opening/funding the account. Since no advertisement on this page states a bonus in connection with a deposit account product, the IF-condition of R-04 is not triggered. Strongest counter-reading: one might argue the 'Full Service $100 Back Offer' or 'Refer a Friend... $500' rewards are 'bonuses' potentially triggering R-04. I rejected this because R-04 as a bank-partnership marketing rule (paired with APY, minimum balance, and account-opening requirements) is specifically about deposit account bonuses (e.g., 'open a checking account and get a $200 bonus'), not tax-prep service credits or referral rewards unconnected to any deposit account. There is no APY, no account minimum balance discussion, nor any deposit account opening incentive language anywhere on this page tied to a monetary bonus. Therefore the rule does not apply.

Rule R-04 applies only if the advertisement on the page states a bonus on a deposit account (e.g., a bank account opening bonus). The entire page is about TurboTax tax preparation products, guarantees, pricing, and some Credit Karma Money checking account disclosures, but there is no advertised bonus amount or general bonus language tied to opening or using a checking or savings account. The only uses of the word “Bonus” are in the link text “Bonus tax calculator,” which relates to employment bonuses as taxable income, not a bank account bonus. Therefore the rule’s trigger condition is not met, so the rule is not applicable to this page.

The page promotes TurboTax services and includes promotions like a $100 back offer on TurboTax Full Service and referral rewards, plus information about Credit Karma Money account features. There is no advertisement of a deposit account bonus that would invoke Truth in Savings-style bonus disclosures (APY, balance requirements, timing of bonus). The cited offers are service credits/rewards, not deposit account bonuses. Therefore, the rule’s trigger is not met.
